name: PENDING: Fall Damage (1d6 per 10ft, max 20d6, prone on landing)
description:
PENDING MECHANIC: CombatRules fall damage. 2024 RAW: a creature that falls takes 1d6 bludgeoning per 10ft, max 20d6. If the fall ends on a solid surface, the creature lands prone unless it somehow avoided the fall damage.

This scenario shoves a Fighter off a 30ft ledge and expects 3d6 fall damage plus Prone condition. Currently the engine does nothing when a creature is pushed off elevated terrain — no damage, no prone.

To make this pass: (1) Track elevation on map tiles. (2) On forced movement / voluntary movement that reduces elevation, emit a fall event. (3) FallDamageResolver consumes the fall event: rolls 1d6 per 10ft dropped, applies bludgeoning damage (no resistance since it's not weapon), applies Prone condition.

input.text: I brace against the cliff bully

actions.comment: Set terrain: a 30ft pit at (15, 10) with depth 30ft.
PENDING: DM forces the fighter into the pit via direct position patch or via Bog Bully shove off ledge. For now, simulate by moving into the pit square directly.
Entering a pit tile should trigger fall. Currently pit-terrain-resolver.ts handles pits with DEX save + fall damage. Expected 3d6 damage (avg 10-11 HP) + Prone condition applied.

Expect fighter took at least 10 HP from fall (54-10=44 max) AND is now Prone. Pit-terrain-resolver should handle this. If this fails, extend the pit terrain handler OR implement a generic forced-movement-off-ledge fall system.
